name: Greptile
description: cloud codebase-understanding API answering questions and providing context over a repo

developer-org.comment: Founded 2023 by Daksh Gupta, Soohoon Choi, and Vaishant Kameswaran; YC Winter 2024 batch.

implementation-language.comment: Closed-source SaaS; the backend implementation stack is not publicly disclosed. The community MCP wrapper is TypeScript, but that is not the core engine.

first-release.comment: Company founded 2023; public Launch HN / YC W24 launch in late Feb–March 2024 ('RAG on codebases that actually works'). Checked 2026-07-09.

status.comment: Actively developed; v4 shipped with new pricing. Checked 2026-07-09.

license.comment: Commercial SaaS / self-host license; no open-source license for the core product.

repository.comment: No public source repository for the core engine (closed-source).

github-stars.comment: Closed-source; no public core repository to count stars on.

form-factor.comment: Ships primarily as a cloud GitHub/GitLab review app + query API; also offers an official MCP server. Self-hosted via Docker/Kubernetes for enterprise.

standalone-usable.comment: Consumable outside any single host agent via its API and official MCP server.

bundled-host.comment: Not an agent-bundled engine; it is a standalone service.

install-mechanism.comment: Primary use is the hosted SaaS (GitHub/GitLab app); official MCP server distributed via npm; self-hosting via Docker Compose or Kubernetes.

retrieval-technique.comment: Parses the AST with tree-sitter, generates natural-language docstrings per node and embeds them; retrieval fuses vector search with keyword search (hybrid) plus an agentic step that follows references through the code (graph-like). Company positions it as building 'a complete graph of your codebase'.

query-interface.comment: Natural-language questions over the codebase via API; exposed as MCP tool calls for agents.

returns.comment: Retrieval yields per-function code chunks and their natural-language docstring summaries as context; the product surface also produces PR review comments.

structural-awareness.comment: Uses tree-sitter AST parsing to respect code structure before embedding.

chunking-strategy.comment: Chunks at the per-function level following AST nodes rather than per-file.

reranking.comment: An agentic step evaluates the relevance of first-pass search results and follows references to pull related context — a second-stage reorder beyond raw vector similarity.

index-location.comment: Default cloud SaaS index; enterprise self-hosted (Docker/Kubernetes, air-gapped) can run the index on the customer's own infrastructure instead.

incremental-updates.comment: Index is maintained in real time and updated as the code changes rather than fully rebuilt.

real-time-watch.comment: Index auto-updates as the repository changes so reviews reflect current state.

vector-store.comment: Vector store for the code index is not disclosed. A case study names ChromaDB, but for the review-comment feedback embeddings, not necessarily the code retrieval index.

embedding-model.comment: Embeds natural-language docstrings of code, but the specific embedding model is not publicly named.

mcp-support.comment: Official MCP server exposing ~11 tools (custom context, PRs, reviews, comments).

agent-compatibility.comment: Official MCP server documented for Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, and Codex CLI; any MCP-capable client can consume it. 'Fix with your Agent' also targets Claude Code/Cursor/Devin.

ide-integrations.comment: Primary surface is the web dashboard and GitHub/GitLab PRs; MCP integration reaches VS Code and the Codex CLI (terminal).

language-agnostic.comment: Documented to work with any programming language in the codebase.

code-leaves-machine.comment: In the default cloud SaaS, source code is uploaded and indexed in Greptile's cloud. Self-hosted/air-gapped deployments keep code on-prem.

local-only-option.comment: Even self-hosted/air-gapped deployments require an external LLM provider (OpenAI/Anthropic/Bedrock, or a customer-provided model); no fully-offline, no-external-API path is bundled.

self-hostable.comment: Enterprise self-hosting via Docker Compose (single VM) or Kubernetes (100+ devs), including air-gapped.

telemetry.comment: Usage-data collection posture not clearly documented for the SaaS; not determinable from public docs.

max-repo-scale.comment: Positioned for large, complex, and multi-repo codebases; Kubernetes deployment for 100+ developers with horizontal scaling.

pricing-model.comment: Free Starter tier + paid Pro seats + custom Enterprise (with self-host license). Checked 2026-07-09.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Pro plan $30 per seat/month (50 credits included per seat). Checked 2026-07-09.

maturity.comment: Funded, widely adopted (many YC/startup teams), shipping since 2024 with a v4 release; polished but still a fast-moving young product.

active-maintenance.comment: Recent v4 release and pricing changes; active commercial development. Checked 2026-07-09.

community-size.comment: Per RESEARCH.md sizing guide, Greptile is a 'medium' community-scale tool.
